Part B.key_evidence: Point-like candidate (classtar=0.983, fwhm=3.39 px) with positive subtraction at r~18.3. Nearest PS1 source is 9.2" away (distpsnr1=9.21), so its high sgscore1/magnitudes describe an unrelated neighbor, not the candidate; no obvious host at the exact center of the reference. ndethist=2 with deltajd=0 (all detections within a single night) at a position covered 1617 times. Cutouts show a point-like residual in the science/difference panel without a persistent counterpart in the reference.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Most consistent with a solar-system (asteroid) detection: a clean point source that appears in science but not in the reference, with only same-night detections (deltajd=0, ndethist=2) at a heavily-covered sky position where no previous variability has been recorded, and no coincident host/star within the PSF.
Part B.alternative_analysis: Alternative is a young extragalactic transient (supernova) on a faint/undetected host, or a faint stellar variable; however, the absence of a coincident PS1 source within the PSF and the single-night, two-detection history with no prior activity across 1617 coverages favors a moving object over a stationary astrophysical source.
